summary:
Vocimagene amiretrorepvec is an investigational lentiviral vector-based cancer vaccine developed specifically for patients with recurrent high-grade glioma. While not yet proven through large-scale randomized controlled trials, its design aligns closely with ongoing efforts targeting immune response enhancement via vaccination in this patient group.

---

(End of Round)

We are asked only about effectiveness — so we must focus strictly on whether it has been shown to be effective or if there’s any indication from available data that supports efficacy.

The provided text does NOT state that vocimagene amiretrorepvec demonstrates clinically meaningful effectiveness; rather, it describes it as "investigational" and notes alignment with treatment trends without citing actual outcomes such as survival benefit or tumor reduction.

Therefore:

Vocimagene amiretrorepvec is currently under evaluation but lacks established proof of effectiveness for recurrent high-grade glioma based on existing published evidence. Its potential lies in early-phase development exploring immune activation mechanisms—not confirmed therapeutic success.
